title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week brought significant improvements to OpsPal's Windows installation experience and Salesforce integration reliability. We've streamlined the setup process with native Windows support and enhanced our RevOps assessment capabilities to better detect your organization's revenue fields and sales processes. Additionally, we've strengthened security measures and improved platform stability across all integrations.

entries.headline: Salesforce permissions now handle complex role assignments correctly
Resolved data access restrictions that blocked legitimate operations
details: Added bypass options for authorized tasks
Narrowed overly strict data modification rules
Improved Salesforce permission handling

entries.headline: Quick record lookups now work without extra approval steps
details: Single record searches complete faster
Simple verification queries run immediately
Short data requests bypass complex routing

entries.headline: Windows users can now install OpsPal with native PowerShell commands
details: One-command installation for Git, Claude, and Node.js
No need for separate download steps
Streamlined setup process for Windows environments

entries.headline: OpsPal now detects and reports integration health issues automatically
details: Identifies connection problems before they affect your work
Provides clear status updates on Salesforce access
Validates organization settings during startup

entries.headline: PowerShell installation now works with copy-paste commands
details: Fixed formatting issues that prevented one-line installation
Improved error handling during setup
Installation no longer closes your PowerShell window unexpectedly

entries.headline: Read-only Salesforce queries now work on production environments
Revenue analysis results now save automatically for future reference
You can now customize templates and branding without losing changes during updates
details: Your customizations persist through OpsPal upgrades
Templates and brand assets stay personalized
Easy management of custom resources

entries.headline: RevOps assessments now find your organization's specific revenue fields
details: Automatically detects custom revenue and contract fields
Identifies your active sales processes
Supports non-standard field names like Units, ARR, and ACV

entries.headline: Windows setup now prefers Git Bash over other command line tools
Installation now includes all required dependencies automatically
Added native Windows PowerShell support for easier installation
Setup process is now more user-friendly with better project detection
Added Salesforce deployment safety checks to prevent configuration errors
details: Validates picklist and record type relationships
Checks field history tracking limits
Prevents conflicting configuration deployments

entries.headline: Added automatic scanning to prevent accidental credential commits
Enhanced security scanning for sensitive data protection
Removed embedded credentials for improved security
